all-for-authid.description: Whether to return all ACL entries for the exact current authid only. All ACL entries will appear as `AclUgidType::User` ACLs, regardles of whether they are stored as user or group entries. Hence, when using this parameter this endpoint cannot be used to retrieve information for updating the ACL tree directly.

permissions.description: Returns all ACLs if a user has sufficient privileges on this endpoint. Otherwise it is limited to the user's API tokens. However, if `all-for-authid` is specified, all ACLs of the current Authid will be returned, whether the Authid has privileges to list other ACLs here or not.

POST.description:
\
    Handles the system information of a new machine to install.

    See also
    <https://pve.proxmox.com/wiki/Automated_Installation#Answer_Fetched_via_HTTP>

parameters.description:
\
    Handles the system information of a new machine to install.

    See also
    <https://pve.proxmox.com/wiki/Automated_Installation#Answer_Fetched_via_HTTP>

$schema.description: Metadata of the HTTP POST payload, such as schema version of the document.

version.description:
major.minor version describing the schema version of this document, in a semanticy-version
way.

major: Incremented for incompatible/breaking API changes, e.g. removing an existing
field.
minor: Incremented when adding functionality in a backwards-compatible matter, e.g.
adding a new field.

dmi.description: Collection of various DMI information categories.

system.description: Information about the hardware itself, mostly identifiers.

isorelease.description: Version of the ISO itself, e.g. the spin.

network_interfaces.description: Network devices present on the system.

product.description: The per-product configuration of the installer.

enable_btrfs.description: Whether to enable installations on Btrfs.

product.description: The name of the product.

permissions.description: Implemented through specialized bearer tokens.

returns.description:
Top-level answer file structure, describing all possible options for an
automated installation.

disk-setup.description: Disk configuration for the target installation.

compress.description:
Possible compression algorithms usable with Btrfs. See the accompanying
mount option in btrfs(5).

hdsize.description:
Size of the root partition. Optional, will be heuristically determined if
unset.

filter.description:
Filter against udev properties to select the disks for the installation,
to allow dynamic selection of disks.

filter-match.description:
Whether the associated filters must all match for a device or if any one
is enough.

lvm.description: LVM-specific filesystem options, when using ext4 or xfs as filesystem.

hdsize.description:
Size of the root disk to use, can be used to reserve free space on the
hard disk for further partitioning after the installation. Optional,
will be heuristically determined if unset.

maxroot.description:
Maximum size the `root` volume. Optional, will be heuristically determined
if unset.

maxvz.description:
Maximum size the `data` volume. Optional, will be heuristically determined
if unset.

minfree.description:
Minimum amount of free space that should be left in the LVM volume group.
Optional, will be heuristically determined if unset.

swapsize.description:
Size of the swap volume. Optional, will be heuristically determined if
unset.

arc-max.description: Maximum ARC size that ZFS should use, in MiB.

ashift.description: `ashift` value to create the zpool with.

checksum.description: Possible checksum algorithms usable with ZFS.

compress.description: Possible checksum algorithms usable with ZFS.

copies.description: `copies` value to create the zpool with.

hdsize.description:
Size of the root disk to use, can be used to reserve free space on the
hard disk for further partitioning after the installation. Optional,
will be heuristically determined if unset.

first-boot.description:
Describes from where to fetch the first-boot hook script, either being baked into the ISO or
from a URL.

cert-fingerprint.description: SHA256 cert fingerprint if certificate pinning should be used, if source == "from-url".

ordering.description:
Possible orderings for the `proxmox-first-boot` systemd service.

Determines the final value of `Unit.Before` and `Unit.Wants` in the service
file.

source.description: Possible sources for the optional first-boot hook script/executable file.

url.description: Retrieve the post-install script from a URL, if source == "from-url".

global.description:
General target system options for setting up the system in an automated
installation.

country.description: Country to use for apt mirrors.

fqdn.description: Either a FQDN as string or an object describing the retrieval method.

oneOf.description: Extended configuration for retrieving the FQDN from external sources.

domain.description: Domain to use if none is received via DHCP.

source.description: Describes the source to retrieve the FQDN of the installation.

oneOf.title: from-dhcp

reboot-mode.description: Action to take after the installation completed successfully.

reboot-on-error.description:
Whether to reboot the machine if an error occurred during the
installation.

root-password.description:
Password to set for the `root` PAM account in plain text. Mutual
exclusive with the `root-password-hashed` option.

root-password-hashed.description:
Password to set for the `root` PAM account as hash, created using e.g.
mkpasswd(8). Mutual exclusive with the `root-password` option.

root-ssh-keys.description: Public SSH keys to set up for the `root` PAM account.

subscription-key.description: A Proxmox subscription key for the installed product.

timezone.description: Timezone to set on the new system.

network.description:
Network configuration to set up inside the target installation.
It can either be given statically or taken from the DHCP lease.

oneOf.description: Static network configuration given by the user.

cidr.description: IP address (IPv4 or IPv6) with netmask (CIDR notation).

filter.description: Filter for network devices, to select a specific management interface.

interface-name-pinning.description:
Options controlling the behaviour of the network interface pinning (by
creating appropriate systemd.link files) during the installation.

enabled.description: Whether interfaces should be pinned during the installation.

mapping.description: Maps MAC address to custom name

oneOf.title: from-answer

oneOf.description: Use the network configuration received from the DHCP server.

interface-name-pinning.description:
Options controlling the behaviour of the network interface pinning (by
creating appropriate systemd.link files) during the installation.

enabled.description: Whether interfaces should be pinned during the installation.

mapping.description: Maps MAC address to custom name

oneOf.title: from-dhcp

Configuration for the post-installation hook, which runs after an
installation has completed successfully.

Optional opaque authentication token. The post-hook executor sends
this back to the configured URL as a top-level "token" field so the
receiving side can authenticate the callback.

cert-fingerprint.description: SHA256 cert fingerprint if certificate pinning should be used.

url.description: URL to send a POST request to

target-filter.description:
Map of filters for matching against a property in [`answer::fetch::AnswerFetchData`].
The keys are JSON Pointers as per [RFC6901], the values globs as accepted
by the [glob] crate.

Used to check this configuration against incoming automated installation requests. If this
is unset, it will match any installation not matched "narrower" by other prepared
configurations, thus being the default.

[RFC6901] https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6901
[glob crate] https://docs.rs/glob/

returns.description: Ceph OSD flags and their state.

GET.description: List the cluster's CephFS file systems.

parameters.description: List the cluster's CephFS file systems.

returns.description: CephFS file systems of the cluster.

data_pool.description:
Name of the filesystem's first data pool. A CephFS can have more than
one data pool; consumers interested in the full set should read
'data_pools' instead. Kept for backwards compatibility.

data_pool_ids.description: Numeric ids of the data pools.

data_pools.description:
Names of all data pools assigned to the filesystem; a CephFS can have
multiple data pools (e.g. replicated metadata plus EC data, or multiple
device-class-specific data pools).

metadata_pool_id.description: Numeric id of the metadata pool.

GET.description: List the cluster's Ceph metadata servers (MDS).

parameters.description: List the cluster's Ceph metadata servers (MDS).

returns.description: Ceph metadata servers of the cluster.

addr.description:
Address as advertised by the MDS; Ceph-formatted (typically
'IP:PORT/NONCE').

ceph_version.description: Full Ceph version string of the MDS daemon.

ceph_version_short.description: Short Ceph version string of the MDS daemon (e.g. '19.2.0').

direxists.description: Set when the MDS's data directory exists on this node.

fs_name.description:
Name of the CephFS this MDS is bound to; absent or null for standby
MDSes not currently serving a rank.

host.description: Host the MDS runs on.

rank.description:
MDS rank within the file system; -1 for standby MDSes not currently
bound to a rank.

service.description:
Set if a ceph-mds@<id> systemd unit is enabled on the hosting node;
absent otherwise.

standby_replay.description:
If true, the standby MDS is polling the active MDS for faster recovery
(hot standby).

state.description:
MDS state: Ceph-reported run state (e.g. 'up:active', 'up:standby',
'up:standby-replay') for daemons known to the cluster; 'stopped' or
'unknown' for configured daemons not visible to the cluster.

addr.description:
Address as advertised by the manager; Ceph-formatted (typically
'IP:PORT/NONCE').

ceph_version.description: Full Ceph version string of the manager daemon.

ceph_version_short.description: Short Ceph version string of the manager daemon (e.g. '19.2.0').

direxists.description: Set when the manager's data directory exists on this node.

host.description: Host the manager runs on.

service.description:
Set if a ceph-mgr@<id> systemd unit is enabled on the hosting node;
absent otherwise.

state.description:
Manager state: 'active' or 'standby' for daemons visible to the mgr
cluster, 'stopped' or 'unknown' for configured daemons not currently
visible.

addr.description:
Address as advertised by the monitor; Ceph-formatted (typically
'IP:PORT/NONCE', possibly as a messenger-v2 vector depending on Ceph
version and ceph.conf shape).

ceph_version.description: Full Ceph version string of the monitor daemon.

ceph_version_short.description: Short Ceph version string of the monitor daemon (e.g. '19.2.0').

direxists.description: Set when the monitor's data directory exists on this node.

host.description: Host the monitor runs on.

quorum.description: Set when the monitor is part of the current quorum.

rank.description: Rank of the monitor within the mon map.

service.description:
Set if a ceph-mon@<id> systemd unit is enabled on the hosting node;
absent otherwise.

state.description:
Run state of the monitor: 'running' (in quorum), 'stopped' (systemd unit
configured but daemon not visible to the cluster), or 'unknown' (no
rados access).

application_metadata.description: Application tags attached to the pool (mapping of application name to its metadata object).

autoscale_status.description: Raw pg_autoscaler status object for this pool; shape varies between Ceph releases.

bytes_used.description:
Bytes currently used in the pool; absent if no usage statistics are
reported.

crush_rule.description: Numeric id of the CRUSH rule used by this pool.

crush_rule_name.description:
Human-readable name of the CRUSH rule used by this pool; absent if the
rule id is not in the current CRUSH map.

min_size.description: Minimum number of replicas required to accept writes.

percent_used.description:
Percentage of pool capacity currently used; absent if no usage
statistics are reported.

pg_num_final.description: Optimal placement-group count computed by pg_autoscaler.

pg_num_min.description: Minimum placement-group count the pg_autoscaler may choose.

pool.description: Numeric pool id assigned by Ceph.

size.description: Replication factor (target number of object replicas).

target_size.description: Operator-supplied target size in bytes; hints the pg_autoscaler.

target_size_ratio.description:
Operator-supplied target ratio of total pool capacity; hints the
pg_autoscaler.

type.description:
Pool type: 'replicated' for n-way replication, 'erasure' for an
erasure-coded pool, 'unknown' for types PVE does not yet map.

GET.description:
Cluster-wide Ceph status (the raw `ceph status` object), served from the cache within `max-age`
seconds or fetched fresh and cached.

parameters.description:
Cluster-wide Ceph status (the raw `ceph status` object), served from the cache within `max-age`
seconds or fetched fresh and cached.

max-age.description: Serve a cached status if it is younger than this many seconds.

GET.description:
Typed, summarized Ceph cluster status (health, capacity, OSD/MON/MGR/PG counts) for the
dashboard. Most fields come from the cached `ceph status` (so the UI binds typed fields instead
of parsing a raw blob); the fullest-pool and version fields are enriched from a live,
best-effort member read (see [`enrich_summary`]).

parameters.description:
Typed, summarized Ceph cluster status (health, capacity, OSD/MON/MGR/PG counts) for the
dashboard. Most fields come from the cached `ceph status` (so the UI binds typed fields instead
of parsing a raw blob); the fullest-pool and version fields are enriched from a live,
best-effort member read (see [`enrich_summary`]).

max-age.description: Serve a cached status if it is younger than this many seconds.

returns.description:
Typed, summarized Ceph cluster status for the dashboard.

Computed server-side from the raw `ceph status` object so the UI binds to typed fields instead
of digging through an untyped JSON blob. Returned by `GET /ceph/clusters/{cluster}/summary`.

items.description: A single active Ceph health check.

degraded-ratio.description: Fraction of objects degraded, when degraded.

fullest-pool.description:
Name of the most-utilized pool, to surface a near-full pool that the cluster-wide capacity
figure would otherwise hide.

members.description:
The cluster's registered members (the PVE remotes / nodes backing it). From the PDM
registry, not the live status; surfaces cross-remote membership on the overview. Filled by
the summary endpoint.

items.description:
A registered cluster member as shown on the dashboard: a compact projection of the registry's
[`CephMember`] without any connection secrets.

kind.description:
Kind of a Ceph cluster member.

Determines the transport PDM uses to reach this member: `Pve` members are reached via the
existing PVE remote (through PVE's Ceph REST API, or the locally-routed `proxmox-ceph-node-api`
daemon); `Standalone` members are reached via that daemon directly.

node.description: The node name (PVE node, or standalone host).

remote.description: For `kind = pve`: the PDM remote id backing this member.

misplaced-ratio.description: Fraction of objects misplaced, when rebalancing.

osds-total.description: Total OSDs known to the cluster.

items.description: PG count for one placement-group state combination.

count.description: Number of placement groups in this state.

recovery-bytes-sec.description: Recovery throughput (bytes/s), when a recovery is active.

version.description: Representative Ceph version of the cluster (from the monitors).

version-mixed.description:
Whether the monitors report more than one distinct Ceph version (a mid-upgrade /
version-skew signal).

GET.description: List registered Ceph clusters, filtered to those the caller can audit.

parameters.description: List registered Ceph clusters, filtered to those the caller can audit.

permissions.description: Returns only clusters with at least one auditable PVE member.

returns.description: Registered Ceph clusters the caller can access.

items.description:
Summary entry for the cluster-list view.

Aggregates persistent config (display name) with live-derived state (member count, reachability,
last-known health). Returned by `GET /api2/extjs/ceph/clusters`.

bytes-avail.description: Available raw capacity in bytes, from the cached status; absent if uncached.

bytes-total.description: Total raw capacity in bytes, from the cached status; absent if uncached.

bytes-used.description: Used raw capacity in bytes, from the cached status; absent if uncached.

health.description:
Last known Ceph health string (HEALTH_OK / HEALTH_WARN / HEALTH_ERR), from the cached
status; absent if nothing is cached yet.

member-count.description: Total members registered for the cluster.

mons-in-quorum.description: Monitors currently in quorum, from the cached status; absent if uncached.

mons-total.description: Total monitors, from the cached status; absent if uncached.

nearfull.description: Whether a near-full / full health check is active (storage pressure).

node.description:
A representative member node on [`Self::remote`], used to deep-link straight to that node's
Ceph panel. Absent if no PVE member is known.

osds-in.description: OSDs currently in, from the cached status; absent if uncached.

osds-total.description: Total OSDs known to the cluster, from the cached status; absent if uncached.

osds-up.description: OSDs currently up, from the cached status; absent if uncached.

problem-count.description:
Number of active Ceph health checks, from the cached status; lets the list show a problem
count without a per-cluster fetch.

recovering.description: Whether recovery or backfill is in progress (a transient activity).

remote.description:
A representative PVE remote backing the cluster, used to deep-link into the cluster's native
PVE web UI. Absent if no PVE member is known.

state.description:
Detection state of a Ceph cluster as observed by the auto-detection sweep.

`Detected`: at least one probe succeeded this cycle and reported the fsid. `Unreachable`: at
least one probe failed AND no probe succeeded; the cluster might still be healthy from another
vantage point we cannot reach. `Gone`: every probe succeeded but no remote reports the fsid -
the Ceph install was removed or rebuilt with a different fsid. `Tombstoned`: operator-driven
"Forget cluster"; the sweep ignores re-detection until an admin runs the tombstone-reset
command.

DELETE.description: Remove an LDAP realm configuration

digest.description: Prevent changes if current configuration file has different SHA256 digest. This can be used to prevent concurrent modifications.

capath.description:
CA certificate to use for the server. The path can point to
either a file, or a directory. If it points to a file,
the PEM-formatted X.509 certificate stored at the path
will be added as a trusted certificate.
If the path points to a directory,
the directory replaces the system's default certificate
store at `/etc/ssl/certs` - Every file in the directory
will be loaded as a trusted certificate.

default.description: True if you want this to be the default realm selected on login.

filter.description: Custom LDAP search filter for user sync

mode.description: LDAP connection type

sync-attributes.description: Comma-separated list of key=value pairs for specifying which LDAP attributes map to which peat user field. For example, to map the LDAP attribute ``mail`` to peat's ``email``, write ``email=mail``.

format.description: Determine which LDAP attributes should be synced to which user attributes

email.description: Name of the LDAP attribute containing the user's email address

firstname.description: Name of the LDAP attribute containing the user's first name

remove-vanished.description: A semicolon-separated list of things to remove when they or the user vanishes during user synchronization. The following values are possible: ``entry`` removes the user when not returned from the sync; ``properties`` removes any  properties on existing user that do not appear in the source. ``acl`` removes ACLs when the user is not returned from the sync.

user-classes.description: Comma-separated list of allowed objectClass values for user synchronization. For instance, if ``user-classes`` is set to ``person,user``, then user synchronization will consider all LDAP entities where ``objectClass: person`` `or` ``objectClass: user``.

capath.description:
CA certificate to use for the server. The path can point to
either a file, or a directory. If it points to a file,
the PEM-formatted X.509 certificate stored at the path
will be added as a trusted certificate.
If the path points to a directory,
the directory replaces the system's default certificate
store at `/etc/ssl/certs` - Every file in the directory
will be loaded as a trusted certificate.

allow-subdomains.description:
If an `origin` is specified, this specifies whether subdomains should be considered valid
as well.

May be changed at any time.

Defaults to `true`.

id.description:
Relying party ID. Must be the domain name without protocol, port or location.

Changing this *will* break existing credentials.

origin.description:
Site origin. Must be a `https://` URL (or `http://localhost`). Should contain the address
users type in their browsers to access the web interface.

Changing this *may* break existing credentials.

rp.description:
Relying party name. Any text identifier.

Changing this *may* break existing credentials.

allow-subdomains.description:
If an `origin` is specified, this specifies whether subdomains should be considered valid
as well.

May be changed at any time.

Defaults to `true`.

DELETE.description: Deactivate an ACME account.

force.description: Delete account data even if the server refuses to deactivate the account.

returns.description:
ACME Account information.

This is what we return via the API.

account.description:
ACME Account data. This is the part of the account returned from and possibly sent to the ACME
provider. Some fields may be uptdated by the user via a request to the account location, others
may not be changed.

contact.description:
The account's contact info.

This usually contains a `"mailto:<email address>"` entry but may also contain some other
data if the server accepts it.

onlyReturnExisting.description: This is only used by the client when querying an account.

termsOfServiceAgreed.description: Indicated whether the user agreed to the ACME provider's terms of service.

directory.description: The ACME directory URL the account was created at.

location.description: The account's own URL within the ACME directory.

tos.description: The ToS URL, if the user agreed to one.

contact.description: List of email addresses, comma separated.

items.description:
An ACME Account entry.

Currently only contains a 'name' property.

until.description: Display all log until this UNIX epoch. Conflicts with 'endcursor'.

maintenance-mode.description: Maintenance mode, type is either 'offline' or 'read-only', message should be enclosed in "

notification-mode.description:
Configure how notifications for this datastore should be sent.
`legacy-sendmail` sends email notifications to the user configured
in `notify-user` via the system's `sendmail` executable.
`notification-system` emits matchable notification events to the
notification system.

s3-download.description: Byte size with optional unit (B, KB (base 10), MB, GB, ..., KiB (base 2), MiB, Gib, ...).

s3-upload.description: Byte size with optional unit (B, KB (base 10), MB, GB, ..., KiB (base 2), MiB, Gib, ...).

notify.description: Datastore notification setting, enum can be one of 'always', 'never', or 'error'.

prune-schedule.description: Run prune job at specified schedule.

chunk-order.description: The order to sort chunks by

default-verification-readers.description: The number of threads to use for reading chunks in verify job.

default-verification-workers.description: The number of threads to use for verifying chunks in verify job.

gc-atime-cutoff.description: Cutoff (in minutes) for chunk cleanup atime check in garbage collection phase 2 (default 24h 5m)

gc-atime-safety-check.description: Check filesystem atime updates are honored during store creation and garbage collection

gc-cache-capacity.description: Garbage collection chunk digest cache capacity

sync-level.description: The level of syncing that is done when writing into a datastore.

verify-new.description: If enabled, all new backups will be verified right after completion.

cmode.description:
Console mode. By default, the console command tries to open a connection to
one of the available tty devices. By setting cmode to 'console' it tries to
attach to /dev/console instead. If you set cmode to 'shell', it simply
invokes a shell inside the container (no login).

cores.description:

NOTE: If the computer has 2 CPUs, it has a total of '2' CPU time. Value
'0' indicates no CPU limit.

debug.description:

start.

This is saved as comment inside the configuration file.

digest.description:
SHA1 digest of configuration file. This can be used to prevent
concurrent modifications.

entrypoint.description:
Command to run as init, optionally with arguments; may start with an
absolute path, relative path, or a binary in $PATH.

env.description:
The container runtime environment as NUL-separated list. Replaces any
lxc.environment.runtime entries in the config.

features.description: Allow containers access to advanced features.

force_rw_sys.description:
Mount /sys in unprivileged containers as `rw` instead of `mixed`. This
can break networking under newer (>= v245) systemd-network use.

fuse.description:
Allow using 'fuse' file systems in a container. Note that interactions
between fuse and the freezer cgroup can potentially cause I/O deadlocks.

keyctl.description:
For unprivileged containers only: Allow the use of the keyctl() system
call. This is required to use docker inside a container. By default
unprivileged containers will see this system call as non-existent. This
is mostly a workaround for systemd-networkd, as it will treat it as a
fatal error when some keyctl() operations are denied by the kernel due
to lacking permissions. Essentially, you can choose between running
systemd-networkd or docker.

mknod.description:
Allow unprivileged containers to use mknod() to add certain device
nodes. This requires a kernel with seccomp trap to user space support
(5.3 or newer). This is experimental.

mount.description:
Allow mounting file systems of specific types. This should be a list of
file system types as used with the mount command. Note that this can
have negative effects on the container's security. With access to a loop
device, mounting a file can circumvent the mknod permission of the
devices cgroup, mounting an NFS file system can block the host's I/O
completely and prevent it from rebooting, etc.

nesting.description:
Allow nesting. Best used with unprivileged containers with additional id
mapping. Note that this will expose procfs and sysfs contents of the
host to the guest. This is also required by systemd to isolate services.

hookscript.description:
Script that will be executed during various steps in the containers
lifetime.

hostname.description: Set a host name for the container.

lxc.description:
Array of lxc low-level configurations ([[key1, value1], [key2, value2]
...]).

memory.description: Amount of RAM for the container in MB.

mp0.description: Use volume as container mount point. Use the special syntax STORAGE_ID:SIZE_IN_GiB to allocate a new volume.

acl.description: Explicitly enable or disable ACL support.

backup.description: Whether to include the mount point in backups.

idmap.description:
Map specific container UIDs/GIDs to underlying disk UIDs/GIDs for this
mount point

keepattrs.description: Inherit ownership and permissions from the mount point directory.

mp.description:
Path to the mount point as seen from inside the container (must not
contain symlinks).

quota.description:
Enable user quotas inside the container (not supported with zfs
subvolumes)

replicate.description: Will include this volume to a storage replica job.

shared.description:
Mark this non-volume mount point as available on multiple nodes (see
'nodes')

volume.description: Volume, device or directory to mount into the container.

backup.description: Whether to include the mount point in backups.

idmap.description:
Map specific container UIDs/GIDs to underlying disk UIDs/GIDs for this
mount point

keepattrs.description: Inherit ownership and permissions from the mount point directory.

mp.description:
Path to the mount point as seen from inside the container (must not
contain symlinks).

quota.description:
Enable user quotas inside the container (not supported with zfs
subvolumes)

replicate.description: Will include this volume to a storage replica job.

shared.description:
Mark this non-volume mount point as available on multiple nodes (see
'nodes')

volume.description: Volume, device or directory to mount into the container.

mp99.description: Use volume as container mount point. Use the special syntax STORAGE_ID:SIZE_IN_GiB to allocate a new volume.

acl.description: Explicitly enable or disable ACL support.

backup.description: Whether to include the mount point in backups.

idmap.description:
Map specific container UIDs/GIDs to underlying disk UIDs/GIDs for this
mount point

keepattrs.description: Inherit ownership and permissions from the mount point directory.

mp.description:
Path to the mount point as seen from inside the container (must not
contain symlinks).

quota.description:
Enable user quotas inside the container (not supported with zfs
subvolumes)

replicate.description: Will include this volume to a storage replica job.

shared.description:
Mark this non-volume mount point as available on multiple nodes (see
'nodes')

volume.description: Volume, device or directory to mount into the container.

nameserver.description:
Sets DNS server IP address for a container. Create will automatically
use the setting from the host if you neither set searchdomain nor
nameserver.

net0.description: Specifies network interfaces for the container.

bridge.description: Bridge to attach the network device to.

firewall.description: Controls whether this interface's firewall rules should be used.

host-managed.description:
Whether this interface's IP configuration should be managed by the host.
When enabled, the host (rather than the container) is responsible for
the interface's IP configuration. The container should not run its own
DHCP client or network manager on this interface. This is useful for
containers that lack an internal network management stack, like many
application containers.

hwaddr.description:
The interface MAC address. This is dynamically allocated by default, but
you can set that statically if needed, for example to always have the
same link-local IPv6 address. (lxc.network.hwaddr)

link_down.description: Whether this interface should be disconnected (like pulling the plug).

mtu.description: Maximum transfer unit of the interface. (lxc.network.mtu)

name.description:
Name of the network device as seen from inside the container.
(lxc.network.name)

rate.description: Apply rate limiting to the interface

tag.description: VLAN tag for this interface.

trunks.description: VLAN ids to pass through the interface

net31.description: Specifies network interfaces for the container.

bridge.description: Bridge to attach the network device to.

firewall.description: Controls whether this interface's firewall rules should be used.

host-managed.description:
Whether this interface's IP configuration should be managed by the host.
When enabled, the host (rather than the container) is responsible for
the interface's IP configuration. The container should not run its own
DHCP client or network manager on this interface. This is useful for
containers that lack an internal network management stack, like many
application containers.

hwaddr.description:
The interface MAC address. This is dynamically allocated by default, but
you can set that statically if needed, for example to always have the
same link-local IPv6 address. (lxc.network.hwaddr)

link_down.description: Whether this interface should be disconnected (like pulling the plug).

mtu.description: Maximum transfer unit of the interface. (lxc.network.mtu)

name.description:
Name of the network device as seen from inside the container.
(lxc.network.name)

rate.description: Apply rate limiting to the interface

tag.description: VLAN tag for this interface.

trunks.description: VLAN ids to pass through the interface

onboot.description: Specifies whether a container will be started during system bootup.

ostype.description:
OS type. This is used to setup configuration inside the container, and
corresponds to lxc setup scripts in
/usr/share/lxc/config/<ostype>.common.conf. Value 'unmanaged' can be used to
skip and OS specific setup.

protection.description:
Sets the protection flag of the container. This will prevent the CT or
CT's disk remove/update operation.

acl.description: Explicitly enable or disable ACL support.

idmap.description:
Map specific container UIDs/GIDs to underlying disk UIDs/GIDs for this
mount point

quota.description:
Enable user quotas inside the container (not supported with zfs
subvolumes)

replicate.description: Will include this volume to a storage replica job.

shared.description:
Mark this non-volume mount point as available on multiple nodes (see
'nodes')

volume.description: Volume, device or directory to mount into the container.

searchdomain.description:
Sets DNS search domains for a container. Create will automatically use
the setting from the host if you neither set searchdomain nor
nameserver.

startup.description:
Startup and shutdown behavior. Order is a non-negative number defining
the general startup order. Shutdown in done with reverse ordering.
Additionally you can set the 'up' or 'down' delay in seconds, which
specifies a delay to wait before the next VM is started or stopped.

swap.description: Amount of SWAP for the container in MB.

tags.description: Tags of the Container. This is only meta information.

timezone.description:
Time zone to use in the container. If option isn't set, then nothing
will be done. Can be set to 'host' to match the host time zone, or an
arbitrary time zone option from /usr/share/zoneinfo/zone.tab

tty.description: Specify the number of tty available to the container

unprivileged.description:
Makes the container run as unprivileged user. For creation, the default
is 1. For restore, the default is the value from the backup. (Should not
be modified manually.)

enable.description: Enable/disable firewall rules.

ipfilter.description:
Enable default IP filters. This is equivalent to adding an empty
ipfilter-net<id> ipset for every interface. Such ipsets implicitly
contain sane default restrictions such as restricting IPv6 link local
addresses to the one derived from the interface's MAC address. For
containers the configured IP addresses will be implicitly added.

delete.description: A list of settings you want to delete.

digest.description:
Prevent changes if current configuration file has a different digest.
This can be used to prevent concurrent modifications.

enable.description: Enable/disable firewall rules.

ipfilter.description:
Enable default IP filters. This is equivalent to adding an empty
ipfilter-net<id> ipset for every interface. Such ipsets implicitly
contain sane default restrictions such as restricting IPv6 link local
addresses to the one derived from the interface's MAC address. For
containers the configured IP addresses will be implicitly added.

action.description: Rule action ('ACCEPT', 'DROP', 'REJECT') or security group name

enable.description: Flag to enable/disable a rule

icmp-type.description:
Specify icmp-type. Only valid if proto equals 'icmp' or
'icmpv6'/'ipv6-icmp'

iface.description:
Network interface name. You have to use network configuration key names
for VMs and containers

ipversion.description:
IP version (4 or 6) - automatically determined from source/dest
addresses

proto.description:
IP protocol. You can use protocol names ('tcp'/'udp') or simple numbers,
as defined in '/etc/protocols'

nexthops.description: A list of nexthops for the route table entry.

items.description: the interface name or ip address of the next hop

protocol.description: The protocol where this route was learned from (e.g. BGP).

mode.description: Through which firmware the system got booted.

secureboot.description: System is booted in secure mode, only applicable for the "efi" mode.

cores.description: The number of physical cores of the CPU.

cpus.description: The number of logical threads of the CPU.

model.description: The CPU model

sockets.description: The number of logical threads of the CPU.

current-kernel.description: Meta-information about the currently booted kernel of this node.

version.description: OS kernel version with build info

loadavg.description: An array of load avg for 1, 5 and 15 minutes respectively.

avail.description: The available bytes in the root filesystem.

free.description: The free bytes on the root filesystem.

total.description: The total size of the root filesystem in bytes.

used.description: The used bytes in the root filesystem.

returns.description: A list of RRD data points on a PVE remote's storage.

items.description: Single point in time with all known data points for a PVE storage.

GET.description:
Get the status of a qemu VM from a remote. If a node is provided, the VM must be on that
node, otherwise the node is determined automatically.

parameters.description:
Get the status of a qemu VM from a remote. If a node is provided, the VM must be on that
node, otherwise the node is determined automatically.

enabled.description: Set when storage is enabled (not disabled).

shared.description: Shared flag from storage configuration.

content.description: A list of contenttypes to filter for

enabled.description: Only include enabled storages.

storage.description: Only list status for specified storage.

target.description: If target is different to 'node', only list shared storages which are accessible by both.

permissions.description: if `target` is set, also requires PRIV_RESOURCE_AUDIT on /resource/{remote}/node/{target}

returns.description: A list of storages of a PVE remote.

enabled.description: Set when storage is enabled (not disabled).

formats.description:
Lists the supported and default format. Use 'formats' instead. Only included
if 'format' parameter is set.

default.description: The default format of the storage.

items.description: The default format of the storage.

select_existing.description:
Instead of creating new volumes, one must select one that is already
existing. Only included if 'format' parameter is set.

shared.description: Shared flag from storage configuration.

mode.description: Through which firmware the system got booted.

secureboot.description: System is booted in secure mode, only applicable for the "efi" mode.

cores.description: The number of physical cores of the CPU.

cpus.description: The number of logical threads of the CPU.

model.description: The CPU model

sockets.description: The number of logical threads of the CPU.

current-kernel.description: Meta-information about the currently booted kernel of this node.

version.description: OS kernel version with build info

loadavg.description: An array of load avg for 1, 5 and 15 minutes respectively.

avail.description: The available bytes in the root filesystem.

free.description: The free bytes on the root filesystem.

total.description: The total size of the root filesystem in bytes.

used.description: The used bytes in the root filesystem.

POST.description: Call termproxy and return shell ticket

parameters.description: Call termproxy and return shell ticket

permissions.description: Restricted to users

returns.description: Object with the user and ticket

ticket.description: VNC ticket used to authenticate websocket upgrade.

user.description: User that obtained the VNC ticket.

permissions.description: The user needs Sys.Console on /resource/{remote}/node/{node}.

GET.description:
Query the remote's version.

FIXME: Should we add an option to explicitly query the entire cluster to get a full version
overview?

parameters.description:
Query the remote's version.

FIXME: Should we add an option to explicitly query the entire cluster to get a full version
overview?

returns.description: List of basic PVE node information

maxmem.description: Number of available memory in bytes.

ssl_fingerprint.description: The SSL fingerprint for the node certificate.

GET.description:
Get the configuration of a qemu VM from a remote. If a node is provided, the VM must be on that
node, otherwise the node is determined automatically.

parameters.description:
Get the configuration of a qemu VM from a remote. If a node is provided, the VM must be on that
node, otherwise the node is determined automatically.

snapshot.description: The name of the snapshot

affinity.description:
List of host cores used to execute guest processes, for example:
0,5,8-11

agent.description:
Enable/disable communication with the QEMU Guest Agent and its
properties.

enabled.description:
Enable/disable communication with a QEMU Guest Agent (QGA) running in
the VM.

freeze-fs.description:
Freeze guest filesystems through QGA for consistent disk state on
operations such as snapshots, backups, replications and clones.

fstrim_cloned_disks.description: Run fstrim after moving a disk or migrating the VM.

allow-ksm.description:
Allow memory pages of this guest to be merged via KSM (Kernel Samepage
Merging).

amd-sev.description: Secure Encrypted Virtualization (SEV) features by AMD CPUs

allow-smt.description:
Sets policy bit to allow Simultaneous Multi Threading (SMT) (Ignored
unless for SEV-SNP)

kernel-hashes.description: Add kernel hashes to guest firmware for measured linux kernel launch

no-debug.description: Sets policy bit to disallow debugging of guest

no-key-sharing.description:
Sets policy bit to disallow key sharing with other guests (Ignored for
SEV-SNP)

type.description:
Enable standard SEV with type='std' or enable experimental SEV-ES with the
'es' option or enable experimental SEV-SNP with the 'snp' option.

audio0.description: Configure a audio device, useful in combination with QXL/Spice.

driver.description: Driver backend for the audio device.

balloon.description:
Amount of target RAM for the VM in MiB. The balloon driver is enabled by
default, unless it is explicitly disabled by setting the value to zero.

boot.description:
Specify guest boot order. Use the 'order=' sub-property as usage with no
key or 'legacy=' is deprecated.

legacy.description:
Boot on floppy (a), hard disk (c), CD-ROM (d), or network (n).
Deprecated, use 'order=' instead.

order.description:
The guest will attempt to boot from devices in the order they appear
here.

Disks, optical drives and passed-through storage USB devices will be
directly booted from, NICs will load PXE, and PCIe devices will
either behave like disks (e.g. NVMe) or load an option ROM (e.g.
RAID controller, hardware NIC).

Note that only devices in this list will be marked as bootable and thus
loaded by the guest firmware (BIOS/UEFI). If you require multiple
disks for booting (e.g. software-raid), you need to specify all of
them here.

Overrides the deprecated 'legacy=[acdn]*' value when given.

bootdisk.description:
Enable booting from specified disk. Deprecated: Use 'boot:
order=foo;bar' instead.

cdrom.description: This is an alias for option -ide2

backup.description: Whether the drive should be included when making backups.

bps.description: Maximum r/w speed in bytes per second.

bps_max_length.description: Maximum length of I/O bursts in seconds.

bps_rd.description: Maximum read speed in bytes per second.

bps_rd_max_length.description: Maximum length of read I/O bursts in seconds.

bps_wr.description: Maximum write speed in bytes per second.

bps_wr_max_length.description: Maximum length of write I/O bursts in seconds.

detect_zeroes.description: Controls whether to detect and try to optimize writes of zeroes.

discard.description: Controls whether to pass discard/trim requests to the underlying storage.

format.description: The drive's backing file's data format.

iops.description: Maximum r/w I/O in operations per second.

iops_max.description: Maximum unthrottled r/w I/O pool in operations per second.

iops_max_length.description: Maximum length of I/O bursts in seconds.

iops_rd.description: Maximum read I/O in operations per second.

iops_rd_max.description: Maximum unthrottled read I/O pool in operations per second.

iops_rd_max_length.description: Maximum length of read I/O bursts in seconds.

iops_wr.description: Maximum write I/O in operations per second.

iops_wr_max.description: Maximum unthrottled write I/O pool in operations per second.

iops_wr_max_length.description: Maximum length of write I/O bursts in seconds.

mbps.description: Maximum r/w speed in megabytes per second.

mbps_max.description: Maximum unthrottled r/w pool in megabytes per second.

mbps_rd.description: Maximum read speed in megabytes per second.

mbps_rd_max.description: Maximum unthrottled read pool in megabytes per second.

mbps_wr.description: Maximum write speed in megabytes per second.

mbps_wr_max.description: Maximum unthrottled write pool in megabytes per second.

model.description: The drive's reported model name, url-encoded, up to 40 bytes long.

replicate.description: Whether the drive should considered for replication jobs.

serial.description: The drive's reported serial number, url-encoded, up to 20 bytes long.

shared.description: Mark this locally-managed volume as available on all nodes

size.description: Disk size. This is purely informational and has no effect.

snapshot.description:
Controls qemu's snapshot mode feature. If activated, changes made to the
disk are temporary and will be discarded when the VM is shutdown.

ssd.description:
Whether to expose this drive as an SSD, rather than a rotational hard
disk.

wwn.description:
The drive's worldwide name, encoded as 16 bytes hex string, prefixed by
'0x'.

cicustom.description:
cloud-init: Specify custom files to replace the automatically generated
ones at start.

meta.description:
Specify a custom file containing all meta data passed to the VM via
cloud-init. This is provider specific meaning configdrive2 and nocloud
differ.

network.description:
To pass a custom file containing all network data to the VM via
cloud-init.

user.description: To pass a custom file containing all user data to the VM via cloud-init.

vendor.description:
To pass a custom file containing all vendor data to the VM via
cloud-init.

cipassword.description:
cloud-init: Password to assign the user. Using this is generally not
recommended. Use ssh keys instead. Also note that older cloud-init
versions do not support hashed passwords.

citype.description:
Specifies the cloud-init configuration format. The default depends on the
configured operating system type (`ostype`. We use the `nocloud` format for
Linux, and `configdrive2` for windows.

ciupgrade.description: cloud-init: do an automatic package upgrade after the first boot.

ciuser.description:
cloud-init: User name to change ssh keys and password for instead of the
image's configured default user.

cores.description: The number of cores per socket.

cputype.description:
Emulated CPU type. Can be default or custom name (custom model names
must be prefixed with 'custom-').

flags.description:
List of additional CPU flags separated by ';'. Use '+FLAG' to enable,
'-FLAG' to disable a flag. There is a special 'nested-virt' shorthand
which controls nested virtualization for the current CPU ('svm' for AMD
and 'vmx' for Intel). Custom CPU models can specify any flag supported
by QEMU/KVM, VM-specific flags must be from the following set for
security reasons: aes, amd-no-ssb, amd-ssbd, hv-evmcs, hv-tlbflush,
ibpb, md-clear, nested-virt, pcid, pdpe1gb, spec-ctrl, ssbd, virt-ssbd

guest-phys-bits.description: Number of physical address bits available to the guest.

hidden.description:
Do not identify as a KVM virtual machine. Only affects vCPUs with x86-64
architecture.

hv-vendor-id.description:
The Hyper-V vendor ID. Some drivers or programs inside Windows guests
need a specific ID.

level.description:
Maximum input value for the basic CPUID leaves the guest can query -
that is the vendor (leaf 0), family/model/stepping and feature bits
(leaf 1), cache and topology info (leaves 4 and B), and so on.
Higher-numbered leaves are hidden. Setting '30' is a common workaround
for Hyper-V boot failures on Windows guests running on recent Intel
hosts. Only applies when the vCPU architecture is x86_64.

phys-bits.description:
The physical memory address bits that are reported to the guest OS.
Should be smaller or equal to the host's. Set to 'host' to use value
from host CPU, but note that doing so will break live migration to CPUs
with other values.

reported-model.description:
CPU model and vendor to report to the guest. Must be a QEMU/KVM supported
model. Only valid for custom CPU model definitions, default models will
always report themselves to the guest OS.

cpuunits.description: CPU weight for a VM, will be clamped to [1, 10000] in cgroup v2.

description.description:
Description for the VM. Shown in the web-interface VM's summary. This is
saved as comment inside the configuration file.

digest.description:
SHA1 digest of configuration file. This can be used to prevent
concurrent modifications.

efidisk0.description: Configure a disk for storing EFI vars.

efitype.description:
Size and type of the OVMF EFI vars. '4m' is newer and recommended, and
required for Secure Boot. For backwards compatibility, '2m' is used if not
otherwise specified. Ignored for VMs with arch=aarch64 (ARM).

format.description: The drive's backing file's data format.

ms-cert.description:
Informational marker indicating the version of the latest Microsoft UEFI
certificates that have been enrolled by Proxmox VE. The value '2023k' means
that the 'Microsoft UEFI CA 2023', the 'Windows UEFI CA 2023' and the
'Microsoft Corporation KEK 2K CA 2023' certificates are included. The values
'2023' and '2023w' are deprecated and for compatibility only.

pre-enrolled-keys.description:
Use am EFI vars template with distribution-specific and Microsoft
Standard keys enrolled, if used with 'efitype=4m'. Note that this will
enable Secure Boot by default, though it can still be turned off from
within the VM.

size.description: Disk size. This is purely informational and has no effect.

freeze.description: Freeze CPU at startup (use 'c' monitor command to start execution).

hookscript.description: Script that will be executed during various steps in the vms lifetime.

hostpci0.description: Map host PCI devices into guest.

device-id.description: Override PCI device ID visible to guest

driver.description:
If set to 'keep' the device will neither be reset nor bound to the
'vfio-pci' driver. Useful for devices that already have the correct driver
loaded.

host.description:
Host PCI device pass through. The PCI ID of a host's PCI device or a
list of PCI virtual functions of the host. HOSTPCIID syntax is:

'bus:dev.func' (hexadecimal numbers)

You can use the 'lspci' command to list existing PCI devices.

Either this or the 'mapping' key must be set.

legacy-igd.description:
Pass this device in legacy IGD mode, making it the primary and exclusive
graphics device in the VM. Requires 'pc-i440fx' machine type and VGA set
to 'none'.

mapping.description:
The ID of a cluster wide mapping. Either this or the default-key 'host'
must be set.

mdev.description:
The type of mediated device to use.
An instance of this type will be created on startup of the VM and
will be cleaned up when the VM stops.

pcie.description: Choose the PCI-express bus (needs the 'q35' machine model).

rombar.description:
Specify whether or not the device's ROM will be visible in the guest's
memory map.

romfile.description: Custom pci device rom filename (must be located in /usr/share/kvm/).

sub-device-id.description: Override PCI subsystem device ID visible to guest

sub-vendor-id.description: Override PCI subsystem vendor ID visible to guest

vendor-id.description: Override PCI vendor ID visible to guest

hotplug.description:
Selectively enable hotplug features. This is a comma separated list of
hotplug features: 'network', 'disk', 'cpu', 'memory', 'usb' and
'cloudinit'. Use '0' to disable hotplug completely. Using '1' as value
is an alias for the default `network,disk,usb`. USB hotplugging is
possible for guests with machine version >= 7.1 and ostype l26 or
windows > 7.

hugepages.description:
Enables hugepages memory.

Sets the size of hugepages in MiB. If the value is set to 'any' then 1 GiB
hugepages will be used if possible, otherwise the size will fall back to 2
MiB.

ipconfig0.description:
cloud-init: Specify IP addresses and gateways for the corresponding interface.

IP addresses use CIDR notation, gateways are optional but need an IP of the same type specified.

The special string 'dhcp' can be used for IP addresses to use DHCP, in which case no explicit
gateway should be provided.
For IPv6 the special string 'auto' can be used to use stateless autoconfiguration. This requires
cloud-init 19.4 or newer.

If cloud-init is enabled and neither an IPv4 nor an IPv6 address is specified, it defaults to using
dhcp on IPv4.

The special string 'dhcp' can be used for IP addresses to use DHCP, in which case no explicit
gateway should be provided.
For IPv6 the special string 'auto' can be used to use stateless autoconfiguration. This requires
cloud-init 19.4 or newer.

If cloud-init is enabled and neither an IPv4 nor an IPv6 address is specified, it defaults to using
dhcp on IPv4.

ivshmem.description:
Inter-VM shared memory. Useful for direct communication between VMs, or
to the host.

name.description:
The name of the file. Will be prefixed with 'pve-shm-'. Default is the
VMID. Will be deleted when the VM is stopped.

size.description: The size of the file in MB.

keephugepages.description:
Use together with hugepages. If enabled, hugepages will not not be
deleted after VM shutdown and can be used for subsequent starts.

keyboard.description:
Keyboard layout for VNC server. This option is generally not required and is
often better handled from within the guest OS.

localtime.description:
Set the real time clock (RTC) to local time. This is enabled by default
if the `ostype` indicates a Microsoft Windows OS.

aw-bits.description: Specifies the vIOMMU address space bit width.

enable-s3.description:
Enables S3 power state. Defaults to false beginning with machine types
9.2+pve1, true before.

enable-s4.description:
Enables S4 power state. Defaults to false beginning with machine types
9.2+pve1, true before.

viommu.description:
Enable and set guest vIOMMU variant (Intel vIOMMU needs q35 to be set as
machine type).

current.description:
Current amount of online RAM for the VM in MiB. This is the maximum
available memory when you use the balloon device.

creation-qemu.description: The QEMU (machine) version from the time this VM was created.

ctime.description: The guest creation timestamp as UNIX epoch time

migrate_downtime.description:
Set maximum tolerated downtime (in seconds) for migrations. Should the
migration not be able to converge in the very end, because too much
newly dirtied RAM needs to be transferred, the limit will be increased
automatically step-by-step until migration can converge. Will be capped
to 2000 seconds (maximum in QEMU).

migrate_speed.description: Set maximum speed (in MB/s) for migrations. Value 0 is no limit.

name.description: Set a name for the VM. Only used on the configuration web interface.

nameserver.description:
cloud-init: Sets DNS server IP address for a container. Create will
automatically use the setting from the host if neither searchdomain nor
nameserver are set.

bridge.description:
Bridge to attach the network device to. The Proxmox VE standard bridge
is called 'vmbr0'.

If you do not specify a bridge, we create a kvm user (NATed) network
device, which provides DHCP and DNS services. The following addresses
are used:

10.0.2.2   Gateway
10.0.2.3   DNS Server
10.0.2.4   SMB Server

The DHCP server assign addresses to the guest starting from 10.0.2.15.

firewall.description: Whether this interface should be protected by the firewall.

link_down.description: Whether this interface should be disconnected (like pulling the plug).

macaddr.description:
MAC address. That address must be unique within your network. This is
automatically generated if not specified.

model.description:
Network Card Model. The 'virtio' model provides the best performance with
very low CPU overhead. If your guest does not support this driver, it is
usually best to use 'e1000'.

mtu.description:
Force MTU of network device (VirtIO only). Setting to '1' or empty will
use the bridge MTU

queues.description: Number of packet queues to be used on the device.

rate.description: Rate limit in mbps (megabytes per second) as floating point number.

tag.description: VLAN tag to apply to packets on this interface.

trunks.description: VLAN trunks to pass through this interface.

10.0.2.4   SMB Server

The DHCP server assign addresses to the guest starting from 10.0.2.15.

firewall.description: Whether this interface should be protected by the firewall.

link_down.description: Whether this interface should be disconnected (like pulling the plug).

macaddr.description:
MAC address. That address must be unique within your network. This is
automatically generated if not specified.

model.description:
Network Card Model. The 'virtio' model provides the best performance with
very low CPU overhead. If your guest does not support this driver, it is
usually best to use 'e1000'.

mtu.description:
Force MTU of network device (VirtIO only). Setting to '1' or empty will
use the bridge MTU

queues.description: Number of packet queues to be used on the device.

rate.description: Rate limit in mbps (megabytes per second) as floating point number.

tag.description: VLAN tag to apply to packets on this interface.

trunks.description: VLAN trunks to pass through this interface.

bridge.description:
Bridge to attach the network device to. The Proxmox VE standard bridge
is called 'vmbr0'.

If you do not specify a bridge, we create a kvm user (NATed) network
device, which provides DHCP and DNS services. The following addresses
are used:

10.0.2.2   Gateway
10.0.2.3   DNS Server
10.0.2.4   SMB Server

The DHCP server assign addresses to the guest starting from 10.0.2.15.

memory.description: Amount of memory this NUMA node provides.

onboot.description: Specifies whether a VM will be started during system bootup.

parallel0.description: Map host parallel devices (n is 0 to 2).

parallel1.description: Map host parallel devices (n is 0 to 2).

parallel2.description: Map host parallel devices (n is 0 to 2).

parent.description:
Parent snapshot name. This is used internally, and should not be
modified.

protection.description:
Sets the protection flag of the VM. This will disable the remove VM and
remove disk operations.

reboot.description: Allow reboot. If set to '0' the VM exit on reboot.

rng0.description: Configure a VirtIO-based Random Number Generator.

max_bytes.description:
Maximum bytes of entropy allowed to get injected into the guest every
'period' milliseconds. Use `0` to disable limiting (potentially
dangerous!).

period.description:
Every 'period' milliseconds the entropy-injection quota is reset,
allowing the guest to retrieve another 'max_bytes' of entropy.

source.description:
The file on the host to gather entropy from. Using urandom does *not*
decrease security in any meaningful way, as it's still seeded from real
entropy, and the bytes provided will most likely be mixed with real entropy
on the guest as well. '/dev/hwrng' can be used to pass through a hardware
RNG from the host.

running-nets-host-mtu.description:
List of VirtIO network devices and their effective host_mtu setting. A
value of 0 means that the host_mtu parameter is to be avoided for the
corresponding device. This is used internally for snapshots.

runningcpu.description:
Specifies the QEMU '-cpu' parameter of the running vm. This is used
internally for snapshots.

runningmachine.description:
Specifies the QEMU machine type of the running vm. This is used
internally for snapshots.

aw-bits.description: Specifies the vIOMMU address space bit width.

enable-s3.description:
Enables S3 power state. Defaults to false beginning with machine types
9.2+pve1, true before.

enable-s4.description:
Enables S4 power state. Defaults to false beginning with machine types
9.2+pve1, true before.

viommu.description:
Enable and set guest vIOMMU variant (Intel vIOMMU needs q35 to be set as
machine type).

sata0.description: Use volume as SATA hard disk or CD-ROM (n is 0 to 5).

backup.description: Whether the drive should be included when making backups.

bps.description: Maximum r/w speed in bytes per second.

bps_max_length.description: Maximum length of I/O bursts in seconds.

bps_rd.description: Maximum read speed in bytes per second.

bps_rd_max_length.description: Maximum length of read I/O bursts in seconds.

bps_wr.description: Maximum write speed in bytes per second.

bps_wr_max_length.description: Maximum length of write I/O bursts in seconds.

detect_zeroes.description: Controls whether to detect and try to optimize writes of zeroes.

discard.description: Controls whether to pass discard/trim requests to the underlying storage.

format.description: The drive's backing file's data format.

iops.description: Maximum r/w I/O in operations per second.

iops_max.description: Maximum unthrottled r/w I/O pool in operations per second.

iops_max_length.description: Maximum length of I/O bursts in seconds.

iops_rd.description: Maximum read I/O in operations per second.

iops_rd_max.description: Maximum unthrottled read I/O pool in operations per second.

iops_rd_max_length.description: Maximum length of read I/O bursts in seconds.

iops_wr.description: Maximum write I/O in operations per second.

iops_wr_max.description: Maximum unthrottled write I/O pool in operations per second.

iops_wr_max_length.description: Maximum length of write I/O bursts in seconds.

mbps.description: Maximum r/w speed in megabytes per second.

mbps_max.description: Maximum unthrottled r/w pool in megabytes per second.

mbps_rd.description: Maximum read speed in megabytes per second.

mbps_rd_max.description: Maximum unthrottled read pool in megabytes per second.

mbps_wr.description: Maximum write speed in megabytes per second.

mbps_wr_max.description: Maximum unthrottled write pool in megabytes per second.

replicate.description: Whether the drive should considered for replication jobs.

serial.description: The drive's reported serial number, url-encoded, up to 20 bytes long.

shared.description: Mark this locally-managed volume as available on all nodes

snapshot.description:
Controls qemu's snapshot mode feature. If activated, changes made to the
disk are temporary and will be discarded when the VM is shutdown.

Whether to expose this drive as an SSD, rather than a rotational hard
disk.

'0x'.

backup.description: Whether the drive should be included when making backups.

discard.description: Controls whether to pass discard/trim requests to the underlying storage.

searchdomain.description:
cloud-init: Sets DNS search domains for a container. Create will
automatically use the setting from the host if neither searchdomain nor
nameserver are set.

serial0.description: Create a serial device inside the VM (n is 0 to 3)

serial1.description: Create a serial device inside the VM (n is 0 to 3)

serial2.description: Create a serial device inside the VM (n is 0 to 3)

serial3.description: Create a serial device inside the VM (n is 0 to 3)

shares.description:
Amount of memory shares for auto-ballooning. The larger the number is,
the more memory this VM gets. Number is relative to weights of all other
running VMs. Using zero disables auto-ballooning. Auto-ballooning is
done by pvestatd.

base64.description: Flag to indicate that the SMBIOS values are base64 encoded

product.description: Set SMBIOS1 product ID.

serial.description: Set SMBIOS1 serial number.

version.description: Set SMBIOS1 version.

smp.description: The number of CPUs. Please use option -sockets instead.

foldersharing.description:
Enable folder sharing via SPICE. Needs Spice-WebDAV daemon installed in
the VM.

videostreaming.description: Enable video streaming. Uses compression for detected video streams.

sshkeys.description: cloud-init: Setup public SSH keys (one key per line, OpenSSH format).

startdate.description:
Set the initial date of the real time clock. Valid format for date
are:'now' or '2006-06-17T16:01:21' or '2006-06-17'.

startup.description:
Startup and shutdown behavior. Order is a non-negative number defining
the general startup order. Shutdown in done with reverse ordering.
Additionally you can set the 'up' or 'down' delay in seconds, which
specifies a delay to wait before the next VM is started or stopped.

tags.description: Tags of the VM. This is only meta information.

tpmstate0.description: Configure a Disk for storing TPM state. The format is fixed to 'raw'.

size.description: Disk size. This is purely informational and has no effect.

version.description:
The TPM interface version. v2.0 is newer and should be preferred. Note that
this cannot be changed later on.

usb0.description: Configure an USB device (n is 0 to 4, for machine version >= 7.1 and ostype l26 or windows > 7, n can be up to 14).

host.description:
The Host USB device or port or the value 'spice'. HOSTUSBDEVICE syntax
is:

'bus-port(.port)*' (decimal numbers) or
'vendor_id:product_id' (hexadecimal numbers) or
'spice'

You can use the 'lsusb -t' command to list existing usb devices.

NOTE: This option allows direct access to host hardware. So it is no
longer possible to migrate such machines - use with special care.

The value 'spice' can be used to add a usb redirection devices for
spice.

Either this or the 'mapping' key must be set.

mapping.description:
The ID of a cluster wide mapping. Either this or the default-key 'host'
must be set.

usb3.description:
Specifies whether if given host option is a USB3 device or port. For
modern guests (machine version >= 7.1 and ostype l26 and windows > 7),
this flag is irrelevant (all devices are plugged into a xhci
controller).

NOTE: This option allows direct access to host hardware. So it is no
longer possible to migrate such machines - use with special care.

The value 'spice' can be used to add a usb redirection devices for
spice.

Either this or the 'mapping' key must be set.

mapping.description:
The ID of a cluster wide mapping. Either this or the default-key 'host'
must be set.

usb3.description:
Specifies whether if given host option is a USB3 device or port. For
modern guests (machine version >= 7.1 and ostype l26 and windows > 7),
this flag is irrelevant (all devices are plugged into a xhci
controller).

clipboard.description:
Enable a specific clipboard. If not set, depending on the display type the
SPICE one will be added. Live migration with a VNC clipboard is not possible
with QEMU machine version < 10.1.

memory.description: Sets the VGA memory (in MiB). Has no effect with serial display.

type.description: Select the VGA type. Using type 'cirrus' is not recommended.

virtiofs0.description: Configuration for sharing a directory between host and guest using Virtio-fs.

cache.description:
The caching policy the file system should use (auto, always, metadata,
never).

direct-io.description: Honor the O_DIRECT flag passed down by guest applications.

dirid.description:
Mapping identifier of the directory mapping to be shared with the guest.
Also used as a mount tag inside the VM.

expose-acl.description:
Enable support for POSIX ACLs (enabled ACL implies xattr) for this
mount.

expose-xattr.description: Enable support for extended attributes for this mount.

vmgenid.description:
Set VM Generation ID. Use '1' to autogenerate on create or update, pass
'0' to disable explicitly.

vmstate.description:
Reference to a volume which stores the VM state. This is used internally
for snapshots.

vmstatestorage.description: Default storage for VM state volumes/files.

watchdog.description: Create a virtual hardware watchdog device.

action.description:
The action to perform if after activation the guest fails to poll the
watchdog in time.

model.description: Watchdog type to emulate.

enable.description: Enable/disable firewall rules.

ipfilter.description:
Enable default IP filters. This is equivalent to adding an empty
ipfilter-net<id> ipset for every interface. Such ipsets implicitly
contain sane default restrictions such as restricting IPv6 link local
addresses to the one derived from the interface's MAC address. For
containers the configured IP addresses will be implicitly added.

delete.description: A list of settings you want to delete.

digest.description:
Prevent changes if current configuration file has a different digest.
This can be used to prevent concurrent modifications.

enable.description: Enable/disable firewall rules.

ipfilter.description:
Enable default IP filters. This is equivalent to adding an empty
ipfilter-net<id> ipset for every interface. Such ipsets implicitly
contain sane default restrictions such as restricting IPv6 link local
addresses to the one derived from the interface's MAC address. For
containers the configured IP addresses will be implicitly added.

action.description: Rule action ('ACCEPT', 'DROP', 'REJECT') or security group name

enable.description: Flag to enable/disable a rule

icmp-type.description:
Specify icmp-type. Only valid if proto equals 'icmp' or
'icmpv6'/'ipv6-icmp'

iface.description:
Network interface name. You have to use network configuration key names
for VMs and containers

ipversion.description:
IP version (4 or 6) - automatically determined from source/dest
addresses

proto.description:
IP protocol. You can use protocol names ('tcp'/'udp') or simple numbers,
as defined in '/etc/protocols'

allowed_nodes.description: List of nodes allowed for migration.

dependent-ha-resources.description:
HA resources, which will be migrated to the same target node as the VM,
because these are in positive affinity with the VM.

items.description: The '<ty>:<id>' resource IDs of a HA resource with a positive affinity rule to this VM.

has-dbus-vmstate.description:
Whether the VM host supports migrating additional VM state, such as
conntrack entries.

local_disks.description: List local disks including CD-Rom, unused and not referenced disks

cdrom.description: True if the disk is a cdrom.

is_unused.description: True if the disk is unused.

size.description: The size of the disk in bytes.

local_resources.description: List local resources (e.g. pci, usb) that block migration.

mapped-resource-info.description: Object of mapped resources with additional information such if they're live migratable.

mapped-resources.description:
List of mapped resources e.g. pci, usb. Deprecated, use
'mapped-resource-info' instead.

not_allowed_nodes.description: List of not allowed nodes with additional information.

blocking-ha-resources.description: HA resources, which are blocking the VM from being migrated to the node.

cause.description: The reason why the HA resource is blocking the migration.

unavailable_storages.description: A list of not available storages.

running.description: Determines if the VM is running.

POST.description: Perform an in-cluster migration of a VM.

parameters.description: Perform an in-cluster migration of a VM.

force.description: Allow to migrate VMs with local devices.

migration-network.description: CIDR of the (sub) network that is used for migration.

migration-type.description:
Migration traffic is encrypted using an SSH tunnel by default. On secure,
completely private networks this can be disabled to increase performance.

online.description: Perform an online migration if the vm is running.

items.description: Mappings of source storages to target storages.

with-local-disks.description: Enable live storage migration for local disks.

GET.description:
Get the pending configuration of a qemu VM from a remote. If a node is provided, the VM must be on that
node, otherwise the node is determined automatically.

parameters.description:
Get the pending configuration of a qemu VM from a remote. If a node is provided, the VM must be on that
node, otherwise the node is determined automatically.

POST.description: Perform a remote migration of a VM.

parameters.description: Perform a remote migration of a VM.

delete.description: Delete the original VM and related data after successful migration.

online.description: Perform an online migration if the vm is running.

items.description: Mappings of source bridges to remote bridges.

target-endpoint.description: The target endpoint to use for the connection.

items.description: Mappings of source storages to target storages.

permissions.description: requires PRIV_RESOURCE_MIGRATE on /resource/{remote}/guest/{vmid} for source and target remove and vmid

parameters.description: Resume a paused or suspended remote qemu vm.

returns.description: A list of RRD data points for a QEMU guest.

items.description: Single point in time with all known data points for VMs.

POST.description: Perform a shutdown of a remote qemu vm.

parameters.description: Perform a shutdown of a remote qemu vm.

PUT.description: Update a remote qemu vm snapshot's description. This is synchronous (no worker task).

parameters.description: Update a remote qemu vm snapshot's description. This is synchronous (no worker task).

POST.description:
Roll back a remote qemu vm to a snapshot. This is destructive: it reverts the guest's disk and
configuration to that snapshot (and its RAM, if the snapshot includes it). Optionally starts the
guest afterwards.

parameters.description:
Roll back a remote qemu vm to a snapshot. This is destructive: it reverts the guest's disk and
configuration to that snapshot (and its RAM, if the snapshot includes it). Optionally starts the
guest afterwards.

start.description: Start the guest after a successful rollback.

DELETE.description: Delete a snapshot of a remote qemu vm.

parameters.description: Delete a snapshot of a remote qemu vm.

returns.description: The list of snapshots, including the current state as 'current'.

name.description: Snapshot identifier. Value 'current' identifies the current VM.

vmstate.description: Include the VM's RAM state, so the snapshot resumes exactly where it left off.

node, otherwise the node is determined automatically.

node, otherwise the node is determined automatically.

agent.description: QEMU Guest Agent is enabled in config.

clipboard.description:
Enable a specific clipboard. If not set, depending on the display type the
SPICE one will be added. Live migration with a VNC clipboard is not possible
with QEMU machine version < 10.1.

diskread.description:
The amount of bytes the guest read from it's block devices since the
guest was started. (Note: This info is not available for all storage
types.)

diskwrite.description:
The amount of bytes the guest wrote from it's block devices since the
guest was started. (Note: This info is not available for all storage
types.)

lock.description: The current config lock, if any.

mem.description:
Currently used memory in bytes. Does not take into account kernel
same-page merging (KSM). Uses information from ballooning when
available.

memhost.description:
Current memory usage on the host. Does not take into account kernel
same-page merging (KSM).

netin.description:
The amount of traffic in bytes that was sent to the guest over the
network since it was started.

netout.description:
The amount of traffic in bytes that was sent from the guest over the
network since it was started.

pid.description: PID of the QEMU process, if the VM is running.

pressurecpufull.description: CPU Full pressure stall average over the last 10 seconds.

pressurecpusome.description: CPU Some pressure stall average over the last 10 seconds.

pressureiofull.description: IO Full pressure stall average over the last 10 seconds.

pressureiosome.description: IO Some pressure stall average over the last 10 seconds.

pressurememoryfull.description: Memory Full pressure stall average over the last 10 seconds.

pressurememorysome.description: Memory Some pressure stall average over the last 10 seconds.

qmpstatus.description: VM run state from the 'query-status' QMP monitor command.

running-machine.description: The currently running machine type (if running).

running-qemu.description: The QEMU version the VM is currently using (if running).

serial.description: Guest has serial device configured.

tags.description: The current configured tags, if any

template.description: Determines if the guest is a template.
